channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Colorado governor walks a tightrope in approaching Trump. Deportations likely to be the first test
item.description: As Democratic governors across the country adapt to President-elect Donald Trump’s plans, some are trying to build a friendly relationship while others are strategizing ways to resist.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn And Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What Virginia's special elections tell us about the bigger ballot box battles to come in 2025, 2026
item.description: Democrats held onto their narrow majorities in Virginia's legislature as they won two of three special elections on Tuesday in the first ballot box showdowns of 2025, which were seen as a gauge of voter sentiment following Trump's White House victory
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden admin appeals plea agreements with Guantánamo detainees, including 9/11 mastermind
item.description: The Biden administration is asking for a federal appeals court to temporarily block a plea deal agreement with three detainees in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Disgraced former US Rep. George Santos seeks to delay fraud sentencing to make more podcast episodes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Disgraced former congressman George Santos has asked a New York judge to delay his sentencing on federal fraud charges until the summer so he can pay off more than half a million dollars in fines by making more episodes of his recently launched podcast “Pants on Fire.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 death row inmates reject Biden's commutation of sentences
item.description: Shannon Agofsky and Len Davis believe the life sentences put them at a legal disadvantage with them planning to seek appeals based on claims of innocence.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on investigations into Donald Trump as an appeals court weighs a challenge to the disclosure of a much-anticipated document just days before the president-elect reclaims office.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NY governor proposes $110 million for new child care centers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Continuing a theme of proposing splashy and spendy new proposals aimed at keeping families in New York, Gov. Kathy Hochul took aim at the lack of child care centers Tuesday. Hochul wants to create a $110 million fund to support the construction of new child care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Sen. Warren presses Hegseth to answer questions about his past actions and statements
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sen. Elizabeth Warren is pressing Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump's pick for defense secretary, to answer additional questions about his past actions and statements before next Tuesday's confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina justices block certification of election outcome in race for one of its own seats
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina's highest court blocked on Tuesday the certification of a November election result for one of its own seats so it can review legal arguments by a trailing candidate who contends over 60,000 ballots that were cast shouldn't be counted.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Outgoing WH official calls for US to bolster cybersecurity workforce by hiring non-degree holders
item.description: The White House’s outgoing cyber czar Harry Coker called more funding, deregulation and opening up cyber jobs to those without college degrees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker Johnson: ‘Intention’ is to increase debt limit in massive Trump agenda bill
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said congressional Republicans plan to increase the nation’s borrowing limit as a part of a massive party-line bill they are crafting that will encapsulate President-elect Trump’s legislative agenda.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland and the Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he won't rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US transfers 11 Guantanamo detainees to Oman after more than two decades without charge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon said Monday it had transferred 11 Yemeni men to Oman this week after holding them for more than two decades without charge at the U.S. naval base in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill O'Reilly was in a 'Cabinet meeting' during Mar-a-lago visit
item.description: While Trump's approach is high-risk, O'Reilly argues it reflects his commitment to his agenda and disregard for political norms.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management will get new interim director
item.description: The interim director of the Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management, who has led the office since it was created, is stepping down.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: British prime minister condemns ‘lies and misinformation’ after Musk attacks
item.description: Elon Musk has criticized Keir Starmer, called for new elections in the U.K. and voiced support for far-right figures in the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau resigns as Liberal Party leader, spelling end to time in power
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump's lawyers ask judge to halt sentencing in hush money case
item.description: Editor's note: The video above is from Jan. 3, 2025. NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump asked a judge Monday to halt this week’s sentencing in his hush money case while they appeal a ruling upholding the verdict.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Congress formally certifies Trump's election win
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden bans new offshore oil and gas drilling in most federal waters
item.description: President Joe Biden is moving to ban new offshore oil and gas drilling in most U.S. coastal waters, an effort to block possible action by the incoming Trump administration to expand offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump blasts the 'corrupt' and 'broken' legal system ahead of his sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday blasted the “corrupt” and “broken” legal system ahead of a Jan. 10 sentencing date in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Legacy of Jan. 6 prosecutions in flux with Trump presidency on horizon
item.description: The Justice Department’s prosecution of the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack — hailed as one of the “largest, most complex, and most resource-intensive investigations” in its history — could soon go out with a fizzle.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Sales of Minnesota blackout license plates exceed 250,000 in first year
item.description: More than a quarter-million vehicles in the state now have the plates featuring a simple design of white letters and numbers on a black background.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Krueger, MPR News

item.title: Trump appears with Italian Prime Minister Meloni at his Florida club
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump made an appearance Saturday with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, who was visiting his Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Pulitzer winner quits Washington Post after a cartoon on Bezos is killed
item.description: Washington Post cartoonist Ann Telnaes resigned after an editor rejected her sketch satirizing tech chiefs, including the Post's owner and Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Funeral proceedings for President Jimmy Carter begin
item.description: A motorcade will leave from Phoebe Sumter Medical Center in Americus, Georgia, to Carter's hometown of Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former FBI agent: Transparency could stop conspiracy theorists
item.description: Gallagher said that while misinformation is "always a danger" in national events like these, "the FBI deals in evidence. Conspiracy theorists don't.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Fight over Florida tuition for undocumented immigrants continues years later
item.description: A Florida senator wants to repeal a law that grants in-state tuition rates to undocumented immigrants. State Senator Randy Fine says he just wants to put Floridians first, but the bill is facing backlash from across the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lombardo's State of the State speech set for Jan. 15
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo will give his 2025 State of the State Address on Wednesday, Jan. 15, at 6 p.m., according to a Friday statement from the governor's office.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden awards the Medal of Honor and Medal of Valor to military heroes and first responders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On Feb. 15, 1951, Army Pvt. Bruno R. Orig was returning from a mission when he found his fellow soldiers under attack in what's now known as the Battle of Chipyong-ni.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: With Board Chair Nora Vargas ending her term, here's who's vying to replace her
item.description: As of Friday, there are four people who have shown interest in running to replace Chair Nora Vargas on the San Diego County Board of Supervisors at the end of her term.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Trump will be sentenced Jan. 10 in New York case, days before his inauguration
item.description: In a decision Friday, New York Judge Juan Merchan noted that his inclination was to not impose a sentence of incarceration.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Republican Mike Johnson reelected House speaker vote after dramatic floor vote
item.description: Republican Mike Johnson has won re-election to House speakership on first-round voting. He pushed past GOP holdout and got a boost from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tougher penalties, AI technology at center of Gov. Tim Walz plan to combat fraud
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz rolled out a proposal and signed an executive order to step up efforts to combat fraud in government programs. Some of it would require legislative action.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News
